response:
Short answer: azr-compliance-v3 implements a dedicated, server‑side bulk remediation pipeline backed by Postgres and a timer worker (not the existing sync_queue), with snapshotting, persisted per‑item status for page‑refresh continuity, recovery logic for interrupted runs, and client polling endpoints so the UI can show live progress without reissuing work.

Key points

- Dedicated data model and worker
  - Two Postgres tables: event_grid_bulk_remediation_runs and event_grid_bulk_remediation_run_items.
  - A 15‑second timer worker ProcessEventGridBulkRemediation runs under its own advisory lock to execute items, instead of reusing the sync_queue.

- Bulk start / run semantics
  - The bulk start endpoint reuses an active queued/running run rather than creating duplicates.
  - When a run starts it snapshots the full deployable subscription set from the shared Event Grid coverage SQL so the run has a stable target set.

- Persistence and UI behavior
  - Latest per‑item statuses are persisted so page refreshes and polling clients see consistent progress.
  - Endpoints: POST /api/event-grid/remediation/bulk to start and GET /api/event-grid/remediation/bulk/latest to read the latest run/items. The frontend posts once and polls the latest endpoint, merging item statuses into deployable cards and showing a “Latest Bulk Remediation” panel.

- Scalability and robustness
  - Items ordered/queried by recency (recency index added) to surface the most recent activity.
  - Worker has environment knobs for Fleet polling behavior.
  - Stale running items are requeued to pending so a worker restart can recover interrupted runs.
  - Avoids expensive recomputation of deployable targets when an active run already exists.

- Fleet / Fleet API auth considerations
  - Remediation calls to Fleet or APIM can be done via backend (ClientSecretCredential calling APIM and polling a statusUrl) or via the browser (using operator MSAL bearer token) depending on the flow and required permissions; the system supports a backend-mediated POST /api/event-grid/remediation/ensure as well as browser-side Fleet calls in UI flows.

Overall, azr-compliance-v3 scales bulk Event Grid remediation by isolating bulk work into durable DB runs and items, driving execution with a dedicated, lock‑guarded timer worker, persisting item state for UI continuity, and including recovery and polling mechanisms so large runs can be started, observed, and recovered reliably.

{"ns": "azure-msp", "task": "architecture", "facts": ["azr-fleet-platform-prod contains send_bulk_operation_callback", "automation_policies_topic_trigger depends_on azr-fleet-platform-events", "Wisdm contains Remediation tasks", "send_bulk_operation_callback targets policy-engine", "AppTraces contains send_bulk_operation_callback", "azr-fleet-platform-events/automation/catchall targets azr-compliance-v3", "AZURE_GOVERNANCE_RULE_ENABLE_REQUESTED targets azr-compliance-v3", "AZURE_GOVERNANCE_RULE_DELETE_REQUESTED targets azr-compliance-v3", "azr-fleet-platform-events/automation/catchall contains AZURE_GOVERNANCE_RULE_CREATE_REQUESTED", "Fleet contains parse_automation_rule_event", "send_bulk_operation_callback targets governance bulk_automation_items", "Fleet uses get_bulk_automation_item_janus_callback", "get_bulk_automation_item_janus_callback depends_on governance bulk_automation_items", "Fleet governance callback 404 fix decided governance bulk_automation_items", "parse_automation_rule_event depends_on governance bulk_automation_items", "Fleet implements ensure_bulk_operation_tracking", "AutomationRuleEvent contains governance_message_id", "send_bulk_operation_callback blocked_by governance_message_id", "prod hosts /api/bulk-operations/callback", "Remediation tasks → uses → Azure Monitor Workbooks", "Remediation tasks → uses → Azure Policy", "Remediation tasks → implemented_by → Custom policy initiatives", "Remediation tasks → targets → NIST CSF", "Remediation tasks → implemented_by → policy-as-code", "Remediation tasks → implemented_by → SOC2", "azr-compliance-v3 → authored_by → FAzureProdEngAutomation", "azr-compliance-v3 → authored_by → janus.prod.2", "Remediation tasks depends_on Azure Policy"], "recent": ["2026-04-09: Implemented dedicated server-side Event Grid bulk remediation in azr-compliance-v3. Design choice: do not reuse sync_queue; instead use Postgres-backed tables event_grid_bulk_remediation_runs and event_grid_bulk_remediation_run_items plus a dedicated 15-second timer worker ProcessEventGridBulkRemediation guarded by its own advisory lock. Bulk start endpoint reuses an active queued/running run, snapshots the full deployable subscription set from shared Event Grid coverage SQL, and persists latest-run item statuses for page refresh continuity. EventGridDetails now calls POST /api/event-grid/remediation/bulk once, polls GET /api/event-grid/remediation/bulk/latest, merges latest item statuses into visible deployable cards, and shows a Latest Bulk Remediation panel.
[specifics: application: azr-compliance-v3; postgres_tables: event_grid_bulk_remediation_runs, event_grid_bulk_remediation_run_items; worker: ProcessEventGridBulkRemediation (15s timer, uses its own advisory lock); endpoints: POST /api/event-grid/remediation/bulk, GET /api/event-grid/remediation/bulk/latest; sync_queue_reuse: design choice = do not reuse sync_queue; bulk_start_behavior: reuses an active queued/running run and snapshots the full deployable subscription set from shared Event Grid coverage SQL; status_persistence: latest-run item statuses persisted for page-refresh continuity; EventGridDetails_client: issues POST once then polls GET /api/event-grid/remediation/bulk/latest, merges item statuses into deployable cards and shows Latest Bulk Remediation panel]", "2026-04-09: azr-compliance-v3 Event Grid remediation was switched from a browser-side Fleet API call to a backend-mediated flow because Fleet requires application permission. The backend now acquires a ClientSecretCredential token for scope api://daf679b9-9134-42a1-9998-9cfac279747f/.default and calls https://apim.mscloud.rackspace.com/v1/customers/{customerId}/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/eventgrid/subscription, polling the returned statusUrl until completion. The frontend calls POST /api/event-grid/remediation/ensure instead of Fleet directly.
[specifics: service: azr-compliance-v3 Event Grid remediation; credential_type: ClientSecretCredential; token_scope: api://daf679b9-9134-42a1-9998-9cfac279747f/.default; apim_subscription_endpoint: https://apim.mscloud.rackspace.com/v1/customers/{customerId}/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/eventgrid/subscription; frontend_request: POST /api/event-grid/remediation/ensure; reason_for_backend_flow: Fleet requires application permission; backend_behavior: polls returned statusUrl until completion; previous_flow: browser-side Fleet API call]", "2026-04-09: On 2026-04-09 in azr-compliance-v3, integrated Event Grid remediation on the Event Grid Details page as a browser-side Fleet Platform call rather than a backend proxy because the browser reliably has the operator MSAL Bearer token while the backend, behind APIM, may not retain the raw Authorization credential in deployed environments. The page now uses metadata.coverage.customer_id (aliased from ddi), requests an optional Fleet-specific scope via VITE_FLEET_PLATFORM_API_SCOPE, calls POST /v1/customers/{customerId}/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/eventgrid/subscription against VITE_FLEET_PLATFORM_API_BASE_URL, polls statusUrl until terminal runtimeStatus, treats succeeded/already_exists as success, maps 404 to not_found, and maps 409 to not_eligible. UI adds per-subscription Ensure Event Grid and Ensure All Deployable actions plus local remediation state badges.
[specifics: app: azr-compliance-v3; remediation_call: browser-side Fleet Platform call (instead of backend proxy); auth_reason: browser has operator MSAL Bearer token; backend behind APIM may not retain raw Authorization credential; metadata_field: metadata.coverage.customer_id (aliased from ddi); env_var_scope: VITE_FLEET_PLATFORM_API_SCOPE (optional Fleet-specific scope requested); api_call: POST /v1/customers/{customerId}/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/eventgrid/subscription against VITE_FLEET_PLATFORM_API_BASE_URL; polling_mechanism: polls statusUrl until terminal runtimeStatus; success_conditions: treats succeeded and already_exists as success; error_mappings: 404 -> not_found; 409 -> not_eligible; ui_changes: per-subscription Ensure Event Grid and Ensure All Deployable actions plus local remediation state badges]", "2026-04-10: PR #76 review feedback on server-side Event Grid bulk remediation produced four concrete follow-up fixes: avoid computing deployable targets when an active bulk run already exists; order latest bulk remediation items by recency instead of ascending id; source the BulkRemediationPanel run/loading state from the same paged query as its items to avoid mixed-run rendering; and make the 'View Active Bulk Run' button a true read/refocus action instead of issuing another POST.
[specifics: pr_number: 76; component: BulkRemediationPanel; followup_fix_1: avoid computing deployable targets when an active bulk run already exists; followup_fix_2: order latest bulk remediation items by recency instead of ascending id; followup_fix_3: source BulkRemediationPanel run/loading state from the same paged query as its items to avoid mixed-run rendering; followup_fix_4: change 'View Active Bulk Run' button to a read/refocus action instead of issuing another POST]", "2026-04-10: Created follow-up hardening for Event Grid bulk remediation after PR #77 review comments. The fix adds forward migration 58 for a recency index on event_grid_bulk_remediation_run_items(run_id, updated_at DESC, id DESC), adds bulk-worker-specific Fleet polling env knobs, and requeues stale running items back to pending so a worker restart can recover interrupted runs.
[specifics: pr_number: 77; forward_migration: 58; recency_index: event_grid_bulk_remediation_run_items(run_id, updated_at DESC, id DESC); bulk_worker_fleet_polling_env_knobs: added (bulk-worker-specific Fleet polling env knobs); requeue_stale_running_items: stale running items requeued back to pending so worker restart can recover interrupted runs]", "2026-04-09: On 2026-04-09 in azr-compliance-v3, redesigned Event Grid Details to distinguish deployment inventory from remediation coverage. GetEventGridDeployments now returns metadata.coverage with deployable_missing_event_grid_count, missing_monitoring_count, and arrays for deployable_subscriptions vs out_of_scope_subscriptions, derived from active customer subscriptions, monitoring entitlement, and Rackspace-managed Event Grid inventory. The frontend EventGridDetails page now shows explicit remediation buckets: 'Ready For Event Grid Deployment' for monitoring-enabled subscriptions missing Event Grid, and 'Monitoring Required First' for subscriptions outside monitoring scope. This resolves ambiguity in the prior 'Subscriptions Without Event Grid' card, which only reflected monitoring-entitled scope.
[specifics: resource: azr-compliance-v3; GetEventGridDeployments: returns metadata.coverage; metadata.coverage.field: deployable_missing_event_grid_count; metadata.coverage.field: missing_monitoring_count; metadata.coverage.array: deployable_subscriptions; metadata.coverage.array: out_of_scope_subscriptions; frontend_page: EventGridDetails; remediation_bucket: Ready For Event Grid Deployment; remediation_bucket: Monitoring Required First; prior_card_behavior: 'Subscriptions Without Event Grid' only reflected monitoring-entitled scope]", "2026-03-27: On 2026-03-27, live prod validation after a large event burst in azr-compliance-v3 showed the register path and queue handoff were working, but sync_bulk_automation_execute was delayed behind serial queue processing until 21:07Z. After it ran, post-21:07 transport was clean: /api/bulk-operations/register returned 201 four times, /api/bulk-operations/callback returned 200 seven times, and there were zero response 500s in api_audit_all for that post-21:07 window. Bulk item state since 19:30Z then settled to a mix of succeeded, invalid, queued, and failed: policy duplicates were suppressed as invalid with reason 'A successful policy/AMA create already completed recently...', but a smaller set of policy items for subscription e4c18fc7-011a-44ee-a1d0-5fad9a5a007a still failed with Azure InvalidUpdateRemediationRequest on active remediations, confirming the remaining active-remediation guard needs to live in the downstream remediation app rather than azr-compliance-v3. Alert items still had a residual queued/failed subset with no last_error in bulk_automation_items.
[specifics: service: azr-compliance-v3; sync_bulk_automation_execute: delayed behind serial queue processing; endpoint:/api/bulk-operations/register response_201_count: 4; endpoint:/api/bulk-operations/callback response_200_count: 7; api_audit_all response_500_count: 0 (post-run); bulk_item_states: mix of succeeded, invalid, queued, failed; policy_duplicate_suppressed_reason: A successful policy/AMA create already completed recently...; failing_subscription: e4c18fc7-011a-44ee-a1d0-5fad9a5a007a; failure_error: Azure InvalidUpdateRemediationRequest; alert_items: residual queued/failed subset with no last_error in bulk_automation_items]", "2026-03-30: On 2026-03-30 in azr-compliance-v3 develop, corrected a bad bulk-operations UI fix. The user did not want parent policy invalid/failed diagnostics copied to child alert rows. Removed the child-alert inheritance changes from web-dashboard/src/pages/BulkOperations.jsx and instead added backend backfill in backend-py/GetBulkOperationItems/__init__.py so invalid policy rows with blank last_error derive a reason from subscription_compliance_comparison and azure_policy_assignments. Added integration test test_get_bulk_operation_items_backfills_invalid_policy_reason and pushed commit ac0fc372 to develop for PR #54.
[specifics: repo: azr-compliance-v3; branch: develop; commit: ac0fc372; PR: #54; removed_change: web-dashboard/src/pages/BulkOperations.jsx — child-alert inheritance changes removed; backend_backfill_file: backend-py/GetBulkOperationItems/__init__.py — backfill added; test: test_get_bulk_operation_items_backfills_invalid_policy_reason; backfill_logic: invalid policy rows with blank last_error derive reason from subscription_compliance_comparison and azure_policy_assignments; UI_issue: parent policy invalid/failed diagnostics were being copied to child alert rows (removed)]"]}
